carrier: Crum & Forster SPC (document says 'Crum & Foster SPC' — typo in source)
notes: Policy Wording (L2) — Certificate of Insurance (L1) not separately available; this Policy Wording is the primary governing document. PPO network name not stated in document. NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW for network identification.

evidence.conclusion: Carrier is Crum & Forster SPC. Document contains typo 'Crum & Foster SPC' on page 8.
evidence.exact_language: Underwritten by Crum & Foster SPC and backed by a Reinsurer rated A (Excellent) by AM Best
evidence.flag: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW — 'Foster' is a known typo; actual carrier is Crum & Forster SPC

evidence.exact_language: Total Annual Maximum for all Accident or Sickness Expense Benefits Age 0-69 Options: $150,000, $250,000, $300,000, $500,000, or $1,000,000

evidence.conclusion: $75,000 maximum (single tier, no options)

evidence.exact_language: Deductible per Injury/Sickness Options: $100, $250, $500, $1,000, $2,500, or $5,000

evidence.conclusion: Plan pays 80% or 90% (two tiers); insured pays 10% or 20%. No out-of-pocket maximum stated.
evidence.exact_language: Co-insurance Rate Insurance pays 80%/90% Insured pays 10%/20%
evidence.flag: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW — whether 80/20 vs 90/10 depends on selected plan tier; confirmation needed

evidence.conclusion: Plan pays 60% or 70%; insured pays 30% or 40%. Significantly lower than in-network.
evidence.exact_language: Insurance pays 60%/70% of Usual, Reasonable & Customary (URC) Charges Insured pays 30%/40%

evidence.conclusion: Pre-existing conditions are COVERED (not just acute onset) with a sub-maximum. Options: $25,000 / $30,000 / $40,000 / $50,000.
evidence.exact_language: Pre-existing Conditions Age 0-69 Maximum Benefit Options: $25,000, $30,000, $40,000, or $50,000 Maximum Benefit
evidence.flag: KEY DIFFERENTIATOR — actual pre-existing condition coverage, not just acute onset

evidence.conclusion: $20,000 maximum for pre-existing conditions
evidence.exact_language: Age 70-99 Maximum Benefit: $20,000

evidence.conclusion: Separate deductible for pre-existing conditions: options $1,500 / $2,000 / $2,500 / $5,000 / $10,000

evidence.benefit: Pre-existing Condition Coverage — Sub-limits per Service
evidence.conclusion: Sub-limits per service type within the pre-existing maximum: Hospital/ICU Room & Board $15K; Hospital Miscellaneous $15K; Surgeries $15K; Hospital Emergency $7,500; Assistant Surgeon $5,000; Prescriptions $500
evidence.exact_language: Pre-existing Conditions Sub-Limits: $15,000 Hospital/ICU Room & Board / $15,000 Hospital Miscellaneous / $15,000 Surgeries / $7,500 Hospital Emergency / $5,000 Assistance Surgeon Expenses / $500 Prescriptions

evidence.benefit: Pre-existing Condition Definition — Stable Medication Exception
evidence.conclusion: CRITICAL: Conditions managed by stable medication (no change in type, frequency, or quantity within 24 months prior to Effective Date) are NOT considered pre-existing conditions.
evidence.exact_language: Pre-existing Condition shall not include any illness, disease, or other medical condition for which the Covered Person takes regular continuing medication provided that there has been no change in the type, frequency or quantity of the prescribed drug or medicine within twenty four (24) months preceding the date upon which the Covered Person's coverage became effective under the Policy, so long as the Covered Person takes such medication in accordance with the medical advice given.
evidence.flag: KEY DIFFERENTIATOR — favorable for Indian travelers with stable chronic conditions (e.g., controlled hypertension, stable diabetes on fixed regimen)

evidence.conclusion: Pre-existing = any condition for which medical advice, diagnosis, care, monitoring or treatment was recommended or received before Effective Date, OR which would have caused a reasonably prudent person to seek medical attention. No explicit lookback period stated (unlike 36-month lookback in Seven Corners).
evidence.exact_language: Pre-existing Condition means an illness, disease, or other medical condition of the Covered Person for which medical advice, diagnosis, care, monitoring or treatment was recommended or received from a Doctor, and/or which would have caused a reasonably prudent person to seek medical attention, before the Covered Person's coverage became effective under the Policy, whether disclosed or not on the Covered Person's application or online enrolment.

evidence.conclusion: $20,000 maximum for hospital room and board (ICU included) — applies within overall Medical Maximum
evidence.exact_language: Hospital semi-private room and board (or room and board in an intensive care unit) to a maximum of $20,000
evidence.flag: SUB-LIMIT — hospital room capped at $20K regardless of plan maximum selected

evidence.conclusion: $20,000 maximum for hospital ancillary services (operating room, emergency room, etc.)
evidence.exact_language: Hospital ancillary services (including, but not limited to, use of the operating room or emergency room) to a maximum of $20,000
evidence.flag: SUB-LIMIT — combined hospital room + ancillary capped at $40K total

evidence.conclusion: Urgent Care: $25 copay in-network; $50 copay out-of-network (then plan coinsurance applies)
evidence.exact_language: Urgent Care Visits $25 copay per visit [in-network] $50 copay per visit [out-of-network]

evidence.conclusion: $50 per visit, maximum $500 total (10 visits at max rate)
evidence.exact_language: Physiotherapy $50 per visit up to a maximum of $500
evidence.flag: VERY LIMITED — $500 total for PT. Same low limit as Safe Travels USA Comprehensive.

evidence.conclusion: $25,000 maximum — very low compared to most competitors ($250K-$1M+)
evidence.exact_language: Emergency Medical Evacuation Up to $25,000
evidence.flag: WEAK — $25K evacuation limit is the lowest among all plans reviewed. IMG Platinum covers up to full plan maximum.

evidence.conclusion: UTI coverage limited to $2,000 maximum
evidence.exact_language: Urinary Tract Infection Limited to a maximum of $2,000

evidence.conclusion: Benefits payable for up to 364 days from date of Covered Accident/Sickness (or end of Trip, whichever earlier)
evidence.exact_language: Maximum Benefit Period The earlier of the date the Covered Person's Trip ends, or 364 days from the date of a Covered Accident or Sickness

evidence.conclusion: First covered expense must be incurred within 30 days of Injury/Sickness date; benefits payable for up to 364 days from that date
evidence.exact_language: Incurral Period 30 days after the date of Injury/Sickness

evidence.conclusion: EXCLUDED — Mental Health Disorders not covered
evidence.exact_language: Mental Health Disorders [excluded benefit #38]

evidence.conclusion: EXCLUDED — any condition directly or indirectly related to or caused by cancer, dialysis, or ongoing and preventive care is excluded
evidence.exact_language: expenses incurred for any condition directly or indirectly related to or caused by cancer, dialysis, or on-going and preventive care [exclusion #39]
evidence.flag: CRITICAL EXCLUSION — cancer-related expenses not covered even for new cancer diagnosis during trip

evidence.conclusion: EXCLUDED — cataract surgeries and eye sickness/treatments excluded
evidence.exact_language: expenses incurred for cataract surgeries, eye sickness or treatments [exclusion #40]

evidence.conclusion: Must notify within 30 days of Covered Accident or Loss
evidence.exact_language: You must provide notification of a claim within 30 days of a Covered Accident or Loss.

evidence.conclusion: Claims handled by Robin Assist: claims@robinassist.com; phone 1-888-207-1694; portal at infplans.com/claims
evidence.exact_language: Robin Assist, 45 East Lincoln Street, Columbus, OH 43215. Phone number: 1-888-207-1694; or email: Claims@robinassist.com.

evidence.conclusion: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW — in-network and out-of-network coinsurance rates specified but PPO network name not stated in Policy Wording
evidence.exact_language: Co-insurance Rate Insurance pays 80%/90% [In-Network] ... Insurance pays 60%/70% [Out-of-Network]
evidence.flag: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW — confirm PPO network name from ID card or infplans.com portal

evidence.conclusion: Non-US residents traveling outside their Home Country. Dependents (spouse, unmarried children age 14 days to 18) covered if traveling with insured.
evidence.exact_language: Non-US residents who have completed the online application form and been accepted for cover. All persons eligible for insurance must be traveling outside of their Home Country and have paid the required Premium.

evidence.conclusion: Principal Sum: $25,000. Aggregate limit per policy: $50,000.
evidence.exact_language: Accidental Death and Dismemberment Up to $25,000 Principal Sum
